description_long: This object is a large, historical cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark, weathered metal, showing signs of age and use. The carriage is constructed from heavy wooden planks reinforced with metal bands and bolts, giving it a robust and sturdy appearance. Metal rings and fasteners are attached to the sides of the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The wheels are thick and reinforced with metal, designed to support the heavy weight of the cannon. The overall design suggests it is a siege or fortress cannon, intended for stationary use rather than mobility.
description: A large, antique cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a reinforced wooden carriage with four solid wheels and metal fittings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon barrel and the squared front of the wooden carriage.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the full length of the barrel, the wooden carriage, and the detailed metal reinforcements and wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carriage and the closed end of the cannon barrel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel and carriage details from the other side.
